summary: Patched /Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md end to end and re-reviewed the plan against the full review response.
markdown_response:
Patched only `/Users/roy/Downloads/data-plan.md`.

What changed:
- Moved `quant-build-execution-ready-dataset` behind `--allow-manual-data-step`, leaving only the execution-ready gate unrestricted.
- Added a required `RefreshContext` covering dates, generated labels, all derived paths, skip policy, checkpoint location, and manifest inputs/hashes.
- Tightened date resolution semantics: Asia/Shanghai, SSE calendar, after-close cutoff, closed-date snapping, explicit `requested_end_date` preservation, and freshness probes.
- Corrected the live-date example around the 2026 Labor Day closure. External check used: SSE 2026 closure page, https://www.sse.com.cn/disclosure/dealinstruc/closed/.
- Added official industry manifest orchestration/deprecation details.
- Defined `--skip-baostock` as reuse-only for audited BaoStock/patch artifacts, with required freshness/status reporting.
- Expanded tests for internal orchestration, stale default prevention, manifest path/hash validation, bypass behavior, partial success schema, endpoint freshness, and checkpoint/resume.
- Added the suggested checkpoint/resume contract and `daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh` mode posture.

Formal re-review result: all six findings and both additional optimizations are addressed in the plan text. No findings rejected. No code tests were run because this was a plan-file-only patch; validation was a full re-read of the patched Markdown plus targeted checks for the previously problematic terms and examples.
resolved_findings: High: execution-ready builder bypass resolved by marking quant-build-execution-ready-dataset internal/orchestrator-only by default and requiring --allow-manual-data-step for direct runs.

High: generated-label under-scoping resolved by adding RefreshContext, explicit per-step paths, manifest path/hash requirements, and stale-default tests across production, ML, execution-ready, ETF proxy, regimes, benchmark, labels, and gates.

High: latest completed trading-day semantics resolved with timezone, cutoff, holiday/weekend snapping, explicit closed-date behavior, endpoint freshness probes, and the 2026-05-02 -> 20260430 fixture.
Medium: PIT industry orchestration gap resolved by adding quant-build-official-industry-manifest to unified orchestration and deprecation policy, with manifest coverage/staleness recording.
Medium: --skip-baostock ambiguity resolved by defining it as reuse-only for existing audited BaoStock/patch artifacts, with failure on missing required artifacts and explicit status fields.

Medium: integration test gaps resolved by adding tests for internal orchestration APIs, no deprecated console scripts in production execution path, explicit RefreshContext inputs, output status schema, partial daily-first success, manifest path/hash checks, and bypass argument behavior.
Additional optimization: checkpoint/resume semantics added with stable step IDs, hashes, timestamps, statuses, and reuse rules.
Additional optimization: command posture added through --mode daily-refresh|monthly-refresh|full-refresh under the single public CLI.
